data.title: LightPath Technologies awarded initial $2.2M IR camera order for U.S. Navy
data.description:
• LightPath Technologies (NASDAQ:LPTH) on Wednesday announced its recently acquired subsidiary G5 Infrared has been awarded an initial $2.2 million engineering development model (EDM) order for infrared cameras by L3Harris Technologies.
  • The contract supports the U.S. Navy's Shipboard Panoramic Electro-Optic/Infrared (SPEIR) Program, with G5's systems scheduled for delivery to L3Harris in the 2025 calendar year.
  • LPTH +1.81% premarket to $1.68.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: JPMorgan Chase's Jamie Dimon says 'Take a breath'
data.description:
The current tariff-induced market swoon doesn't compare with 2008 financial crisis, when the world was massively over-leveraged, JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon said on Wednesday.

To calm the current market turmoil, the government needs to show progress on trade deals, he said.

It's "perfectly reasonable" to say trade practices are unfair, he said during an interview on Fox Business on "Mornings with Maria." But trade agreements don't get done overnight. It will take time to reach trade deals.

"Settle down, take a breath," he advises.

The goal of trade policy changes should be to make Europe a stronger trade partner, to counter China's and Russia's moves to dismantle the post-WWII global order, Dimon said. 

Massive fiscal spending during COVID helped keep the U.S. economy strong. Dimon said slower economic growth was inevitable. He's not yet seeing increased defaults, "but I expect them."

Furthermore, he sees a recession as a "likely outcome."

Still, the U.S. economy is "probably doing the best" of advanced economies, partly because of the government spending during and after COVID.

The U.S. "could be growing by 3% per year" if "we get our act together," he said.

It's hard to say how long it will be before the trade turmoil calms down. "The sooner, the better," Dimon said.

"Don't let this go on too long, because it's causing cumulative damage, especially to the United States," he said.

IPOs are being cancelled, loans are being delayed. It's bad for small businesses, middle-sized companies. He said JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) has lost a couple of bond deals to local banks recently.

"JPMorgan is going to be in fine shape, almost no matter what happens," he commented.

We compete with banks in every country. Hundreds of trillions of dollars per day are moving around the world. Chinese banks are bigger than JPMorgan Chase (JPM) by assets, but not by market cap.

Developing news... check back for updates.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Peloton shakes up C-suite with newly created COO position
data.description:
To position the company for further growth and facilitate rapid innovation, Peloton is shaking up its C-suite and creating new positions within its executive team.

As part of this initiative, Charles Kirol has been appointed to the newly created position of Chief Operating Officer. Kirol comes to Peloton (NASDAQ:PTON) from iRobot (IRBT) where he served as Executive Vice President and Chief Global Operations and Technology Officer.

Within the new structure, Andy Rendich will step down as chief supply chain officer and remain at the company as an advisor through the rest of the fiscal year.

Dion Camp Sanders, the current head of emerging business, will become chief commercial officer, leading Peloton’s (NASDAQ:PTON) efforts to grow first-party retail sales, international strategy, bike rental and sales of pre-owned equipment.

Finally, the company has also begun a search for a chief information officer, reporting directly to COO Kirol.

The refreshed C-suite comes as Peloton’s (NASDAQ:PTON) most recent quarterly results show the company may be finally getting back on track from a post-pandemic slump after issuing an upbeat outlook for FY25 thanks to new innovations, widening gross margins, and improved churn rates.

“We see significant opportunities ahead, but we have a steep hill to climb to reach sustained, profitable growth,” the company said recently in a letter to its shareholders. Unfortunately, with all of Peloton’s (PTON) fitness equipment manufactured in Taiwan, this hill was just made a little steeper with 32% import tariffs on Taiwan.

data.title: Safe Pro subsidiary receives order for advanced drone-based infrastructure assessments
data.description:
• Safe Pro (NASDAQ:SPAI) Wednesday announced a service expansion for its Airborne Response drone services subsidiary.
  • Under its existing multi-year agreement with one of Florida’s largest electric utilities, Airborne Response will now deliver enhanced drone-based critical infrastructure assessment services in addition to its routine monitoring and pre/post-hurricane recovery program.
  • Safe Pro (NASDAQ:SPAI) shares up 2.4% premarket.

data.title: Anixa rises after patent win related to breast cancer vaccine
data.description:
• Anixa Biosciences (NASDAQ:ANIX) traded higher in the premarket on Wednesday after the biotech announced that the U.S. has agreed to issue a new patent related to its breast cancer vaccine technology.
  • According to the notice of allowance issued by the U.S. patent office, the patent covers the methods of immunizing patients against breast cancer following the administration of a drug formulation containing human α-lactalbumin protein.
  • The San Jose, California-based biotech said that Cleveland Clinic has licensed this patent and several others related to the technology to Anixa Biosciences (NASDAQ:ANIX).
  • A molecule found in certain breast cancer tissue, human α-lactalbumin protein has become an easy target in the prevention of breast cancer.
  • The newly allowed patent issued by the United States Patent and Trademark Office “will expand the scope of immunogenic compositions utilized in Anixa's breast cancer vaccine,” the company said.

data.title: Genius Group approves share buyback for 20% of issued shares
data.description:
• Genius Group (NYSE:GNS) said that shareholders voted 97.5% in favor of a share buyback.
  • The buyback is for up to 20% of the company’s shares, which is the maximum permitted by Singapore law to be approved at an EGM and shall be effected in compliance with any and all Singapore, federal securities law and NYSE requirements.

data.title: NV5 secures $15 million California transportation infrastructure contract
data.description:
• NV5 (NASDAQ:NVEE) said on Wednesday that it has been awarded a $15 million contract to provide construction management services for the first phase of the North County Corridor project in Stanislaus County, California.
  • The North Country Corridor project consists of four phases designed to improve regional mobility, reduce congestion, and serve as a key driver of economic growth for the county, which produces four million tons of agricultural goods per year, said the company. 
  • Phase one of the project includes the construction of a modern, multi-lane expressway that will feature a state-of-the-art single-point urban interchange, pedestrian-friendly enhancements, and a series of critical infrastructure improvements. The phase also includes upgrades to local roads and the construction of seven bridges and several mechanically stabilized embankment walls.
  • Construction of phase one is set to begin in May 2025 and is expected to be completed in three years, added the company.
  • Shares of NVEE were down -0.45% to $15.35 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Blackboxstocks merger target REalloys acquires rare earth magnet producer PMT Critical Metals
data.description:
• Blackboxstocks (NASDAQ:BLBX) Wednesday said that its merger target REalloys has acquired 100% of PMT Critical Metals in a share exchange representing about 14% of REalloys outstanding common stock.
  • The transaction closed on March 31, and includes the acquisition of rare earth magnet production equipment, intellectual property, research & development, existing contracts, and clients.
  • The facility based in Euclid Ohio produces rare earth metals and magnet materials for the US Defense Logistics Agency, the US Department of Energy’s AMES National Laboratory and other magnet industry clients.

data.title: Bitdeer Technologies mines 114 bitcoins in March
data.description:
• Bitdeer Technologies (NASDAQ:BTDR) said in March its self-mined bitcoin (BTC-USD) came in at 114 BTC, up from 110 BTC it had mined in the prior month but down from 294 BTC in the same period the prior year. 
  • March's total hash rate under management came in at 24.2 EH/s, up from 20.9 EH/s in February. 
  • Proprietary hash rate came in at 12.1 EH/s as compared to 9.4 EH/s in the prior month.
  • The firm also said it has completed mass production of SEALMINER A1s.
  • Shares were down 1.1% in premarket trade.

data.title: STMicroelecronics in focus as Italy withdraws support for CEO: report
data.description:
• The Italian government has withdrawn its support for STMicroelectronics (NYSE:STM) Chief Executive Officer Jean-Marc Chéry for “poor performance,” Finance Minister Giancarlo Giorgetti said.
  • Shares rose 3.5% in premarket trading.
  • News of the withdrawal was first reported by Bloomberg.
  • STMicroelectronics did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Tensions between STMicro and the Italian government — which owns a 27.5% stake in the company — spike after it rejected adding Italian government official Marcello Sala to its supervisory board. It was reported last month that Sala was likely to join the supervisory board, whose purpose is to oversee the board of directors and advise it.
  • News of Italy's dissatisfaction with STMicro first popped up in February, when Bloomberg reported that the European country was considering replacing Chéry for what it has deemed insufficient performance amid headwinds facing the semiconductor industry.
  • STMicro is also jointly owned by the French government, which also owns a 27.5% stake in the European semiconductor company. The analog chip supplier is a joint venture between France and Italy and is the result of a merger of two-state owned companies in 1987.
  • The company is set to hold its annual shareholder meeting on May 28.
